description: This figure presents a comprehensive phase and microstructural characterization of high (~5%) Al-added low-density medium Mn steel in cast and hot-forged conditions. Panel (a) displays X-ray diffraction patterns confirming the presence of α-ferrite (δ/α, BCC) and γ-austenite (FCC) phases in both specimens, with specific diffraction peaks labeled including α₁₁₀, γ₁₁₁, γ₂₀₀, α₂₀₀, γ₂₂₀, α₂₁₁, γ₃₁₁, and γ₂₂₂. The caption states Rietveld refinement indicates approximately 26% austenite in the cast sample and 29% in the hot-forged sample. Panels (b) and (c) show optical micrographs of the cast specimen revealing a dual-phase microstructure featuring dendritic austenite (indicated by blue arrows) distributed within the ferrite matrix. Panels (d) and (e) present optical images of the hot-forged specimen, where residual dendritic structures remain visible (marked by blue arrows) despite partial dissolution during high-temperature soaking prior to forging. Panel (f) provides a scanning electron microscopy image of the hot-forged sample confirming elongated austenite (γ) grains within the δ-ferrite (δ) matrix. Panel (g) shows an EBSD phase map superimposed on the image quality (IQ) map, with red representing BCC (δ-ferrite) and green representing FCC (austenite), visually confirming the elongated austenite morphology. The figure collectively demonstrates that both processing states maintain a stable dual-phase microstructure of δ-ferrite and austenite, with the hot-forged specimen exhibiting slightly higher austenite volume fraction and elongated grain morphology compared to the as-cast dendritic structure.
